user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Keep 3-phase complementary, center-aligned 20 kHz PWM on GTM TOM1 outputs routed to existing pins P00.2–P00.7, start with U=25%, V=50%, W=75%, then every 500 ms increment each duty by +10% and wrap within 0–100%. Preserve current GTM clocking (cmuFxclk0) and TOM1 usage. Maintain 0.5 us deadtime and 1.0 us minimum pulse. Update Cpu0_Main to wait 500 ms between duty updates.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl to configure TOM1 channels 5–10 as 3 high/low complementary pairs with center-aligned mode. Set deadtime=0.5 us and minPulse=1.0 us. Keep GTM/CMU clock setup (cmuFxclk0) unchanged and preserve pin mapping using IfxGtm_PinMap_setTomTout for P00.2–P00.7. Provide two functions matching the reference API: initGtmTom3phInv() to initialize PWM, and updateGtmTom3phInvDuty() to step and wrap the duties by calling IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl_setOnTime. Insert a 500 ms delay in Cpu0_Main between duty updates (e.g., IfxScuCcu_waitWithWdtService(0.5F)).

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Specialized high-level TOM PWM driver for TC3xx that natively supports complementary pairs, center-aligned operation, deadtime and minPulse while preserving the existing TOM1 channels and GTM clocking. It matches the mandatory header selection and maintains the original multi-phase inverter structure.

runtime_function.name: updateGtmTom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms increment U/V/W by +10% (wrap within 0–100%) and apply new center-aligned complementary on-times at 20 kHz to TOM1 channel pairs.

driver_changes: Replace any legacy/non-TC3xx IfxGtm_Pwm usage with IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl per TC387 template and mandatory header selection.
migration_notes.context_preserved: We retain TOM1 on P00.2–P00.7, keep 20 kHz center-aligned operation, preserve cmuFxclk0 GTM clocking, enforce 0.5 us deadtime and 1.0 us minPulse, and add a 500 ms cadence in Cpu0_Main between calls to updateGtmTom3phInvDuty, exactly matching the user's functional requirements.
